_readme: COPY THIS, FILL IT IN, AND THE BUILD WILL EITHER ACCEPT IT OR TELL YOU WHY NOT.
Every number needs a `kind` and a `source`. The kinds are:
measured     a URL, a book, a works drawing - something a person can go and check
reference    a pixel measurement of an image (tools/refmeasure.py fills these in)
engine       a file:line in the Simutrans source
derived      arithmetic over other facts here, with the formula written down
provisional  a guess. It BUILDS, and every report names it as a guess.
A number with no source does not reach the .dat. A source of 'chatgpt', 'ai',
'obvious' or 'typical' is refused by name. If you invented the vehicle, then YOU
are the source: say kind='provisional' and write 'my design decision' - that is
honest, and it builds. What is not allowed is a number that LOOKS researched and
is not, because that is the one kind of mistake nothing downstream can catch.
name: MyVehicle

length_total_m.source: the one number that turns the reference image into metres

length_carunits.formula: all cars equal, so every joint (L_prev - L_this)/2 is zero
length_carunits.source: simconvoi.cc:428 - see core/convoy.py before changing this

cost.source: a guess, in cents - balance it against the pakset's own stock
